title: Aladdin Homes Catalog No. 29, 'The Venus' (first and second floor plans)
creator: Aladdin Company (Bay City, Michigan)
rightsholder_or_source: Internet Archive scan; Aladdin Co. Catalog No. 29, 1917, public domain by age
research_notes: Page n50 of scan; 'The Venus'. Two floor plans on one page plus a cutaway isometric. Casement windows, exposed rafters; combination gable-and-drop roof noted in text.
research_features: Two-story modest home WITH BASEMENT: text describes a grade cellar door and access to cellar/second floor. First-floor plan (living, dining, kitchen, front bedroom) and second-floor plan (bedrooms + bath) shown side by side, with an arched opening between living and dining rooms.
feature_highlights: two floor plans plus a cutaway isometric on one catalog page
story-and-a-half bungalow with a partial second floor
large 'ROOF' area hatched on the upper plan (open sloping roof)
slightly sloping upstairs ceilings noted in the copy
grade cellar door and basement access under the main stair
central straight-run stair serving cellar and bedrooms
arched opening between living and dining rooms
front porch and three upstairs bedrooms with a bath
supported_examples: rectangular rooms derived from walls with labels and sizes

gap_features.why: the upper floor is a partial story under hatched roof planes with slightly sloping (attic knee-wall) ceilings, which is roof/sloped-ceiling geometry rather than a flat per-room height
the front porch is a roofed-but-open space, not a wall-enclosed room
